subject: [SWFL] Publix closed on a land deal in Lee County…

top_story.title: Publix closed on a land deal in Lee County described as an 'attractive' land buy, with Lee County identified as the hotspot for Publix's latest land grab, reported June 2, 2026.

signals_surfaced: Naples business owner Octavio Sarmiento and his company ASSA Designs LLC agreed to pay a $100,000 fine for using a 'stolen' architect seal in a permit fraud scheme; the state's Board of Architecture and Interior Design approved the settlement at a general meeting on April 21, 2026, as reported June 3, 2026.
Publix closed on a land deal in Lee County described as an 'attractive' land buy, with Lee County identified as the hotspot for Publix's latest land grab, reported June 2, 2026.

A lease has been signed with restaurateur Stefano Frittella at the long-vacant Olde Naples Building on Third Street South, owned by the Camalier family; the building has sat empty for nearly 20 years since Fantozzi's closed in August 2006, as reported June 18, 2026.

A Gulf-front estate in Old Naples sold for $27 million in 2026, ranking as the fourth-highest existing home resale of the year in the city; it was originally listed at $39 million, as reported June 24, 2026.
